title: Phase 1 comparative research
description: Conduct Phase 1 comparative research for the merged OCK+OMO system. Produce the required comparative-research artifacts from the project roadmap: OCK capability audit, OMO capability audit, overlap and collision map, durable-versus-active state authority draft with precedence and join-key rules, preserved strengths list, candidate ownership matrix for core domains, and command/runtime implementation notes for later backbone-enforcement work.

comments.author: root
comments.text: Created prd.md with 5 tasks, 3 success criteria, 6 key file references

title: Command authority audit
description: Research how workflow entry points and command discovery currently behave across repo-local .opencode commands, runtime-discovered commands, built-in OMO commands, and compatibility surfaces. Determine which command surfaces function as true workflow roots, which should be treated as subordinate support commands, and which are only thin adapters or compatibility-only remnants. Focus on precedence, authority, and duplicate-entry risk in the command surface without expanding into broader state-boundary or implementation-enforcement work.

title: Unified state architecture
description: Define one unified state model for the merged OCK+OMO system with durable ledger versus active execution tiers, authority rules, join-key strategy, and migration path for combining .beads and .sisyphus without duplicate durable truth.

comments.author: root
comments.text: Created plan.md: Level 3 discovery, 2 waves, 3 tasks, 14 TDD-style verification steps

comments.author: root
comments.text: Created prd.md with 10 tasks, 6 success criteria, 12 affected files

title: OMO bead/process adapter
description: Adapt OMO/oh-my-openagent to the OCK contract: bead-first workflow, OCK-compatible skill and process usage, and durable artifact/state alignment using /.beads instead of parallel durable workflow state.
